item.title: Redefining Psychiatric Treatment Using Machine Learning
item.description: ML can be used to find neurologically-distinct subtypes of schizophrena.

item.title: Deep Learning to Detect Early Cognitive Decline
item.description: Using Siamese Networks, we were able to use a cocktail of MRI scans and gene sequences to predict people who were in most need of future clinical care.

item.title: Brain Networks Becomes More Disorganized with Age
item.description: For decades, functional MRI (fMRI) researchers primarily looked for “task evoked” activity in the brain by looking for small deviations of the BOLD signal against what appeared to be a massive wall of background “noise”. The signal to noise ratio (SNR) of the task-evoked BOLD signal is small: only a few percentage points at 1.5 T, which is a major contributor to the reproducibility crisis in fMRI studies.

item.title: How I Took a MRI of a Very Small Part of my own Brain
item.description: I was the first person ever to record a fMRI signal from the thalamic reticular nucleus.
